product_summary.one_sentence_summary: Nursing-focused OET preparation for healthcare professionals in the UAE and GCC that emphasizes feedback-heavy writing practice.
product_summary.target_user: Nurses in the UAE/GCC who are preparing for OET for registration or migration pathways.
product_summary.core_pain: Nurse candidates need structured practice, especially for writing and speaking, to clear a high-stakes healthcare English exam.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: Higher OET readiness, better writing performance and a route toward registration or migration.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The product is sold as a direct consumer exam-prep offer.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: Some nurses may be sponsored by hospitals or employers.

budget_owner.reason: The learner often pays personally, but employer or family sponsorship can also fund the course.

buyer_influencers.reason: Registration and migration requirements motivate the purchase.
Employer sponsorship or CPD budgets can influence the sale.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: OET is accepted by key healthcare authorities in the UAE and for residency programs in Abu Dhabi and Dubai.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: OET's UAE country page states that the test is accepted by key healthcare authorities in the UAE and by residency programs in Abu Dhabi and Dubai.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: OET is accepted worldwide by healthcare regulators, educational institutions and visa and immigration services.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: OET's official site states broad global acceptance.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Learners need OET Grade B to register with DHA or DOH or migrate to the UK or Australia.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The reviewed sources confirm acceptance, but not the exact Grade B threshold or that this course's certificate is sufficient for the stated routes.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: The curriculum requires 15-24 corrected letters and a max cohort of 10.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: These are internal design choices, not externally verified market facts.
No public source reviewed supports a guarantee claim for this kind of exam-prep offer.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: Learners need OET Grade B to register with DHA or DOH or migrate to the UK or Australia.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The reviewed sources support acceptance but not the precise threshold or universal requirement.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The curriculum requires 15-24 corrected letters and a max cohort of 10.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: These are course design assumptions, not public facts.
A guarantee is not externally supported and is risky marketing language.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: Doctor-exclusive cohorts never mixed with nurses is a differentiator for this nurses product.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: That positioning belongs to the doctor-specific offer and does not prove this product's value.

regulator_entities.name: Department of Health - Abu Dhabi
regulator_entities.relevance: The UAE OET country page says OET is accepted for residency programs in Abu Dhabi.

regulator_entities.name: Dubai Health Authority
regulator_entities.relevance: The UAE OET country page says OET is accepted for residency programs in Dubai.

regulator_entities.name: OET
regulator_entities.relevance: The exam provider is the key external authority for this preparation product.

framework_entities.name: OET Test
framework_entities.relevance: Healthcare-specific English exam used by nurses seeking route-relevant mobility.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: OET is accepted by key healthcare authorities in the UAE and for residency programs in Abu Dhabi and Dubai.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: Healthcare professionals preparing for UAE pathways

regulatory_requirements.requirement: OET is accepted by healthcare regulators, educational institutions and visa and immigration services worldwide.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: OET Intro to OET Course for Nurses
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Official free intro course competes with paid nurse prep.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: OET free sample tests
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Official sample tests reduce willingness to pay for generic prep and set the self-study baseline.

legal_claim_severity.reason: The offer is exam prep linked to route-relevant healthcare registration and migration outcomes.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: The course can be delivered without formal approval if the team has adequate OET teaching expertise.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Claims about grade thresholds or registration usefulness should be checked against current destination-country rules.

proof_stack.reason: Because free official OET materials exist, learners still need to choose this paid course.
The key proof is whether learners actually pass or materially improve their OET performance.
Writing and speaking improvement should be visible before exam day.

proof_burden.reason: The offer needs measurable exam progress and pass outcomes, but not formal accreditation proof.

dependency_types.reason: The course depends on instructors who can give useful writing and speaking feedback.
The OET value proposition depends on GCC route context.

scope_variance_risk.reason: The GCC audience is fairly coherent, but destination-country expectations still vary.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: Expanding beyond the GCC would require more route localisation and positioning changes.

subsidy_exists.reason: No subsidy or reimbursement route is central to the offer.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: This is sold as a direct consumer offer, not a subsidised training product.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: No funding claim is being made.

market_regulatory_context.reason: The product is not a regulated credential itself, but it sits next to healthcare licensing and migration routes.

claim_language_risk.reason: Grade B and registration wording can easily become stronger than the reviewed evidence supports.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: Learners still depend on destination-country acceptance and exam performance, not just the course.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: Official free OET resources already exist, so differentiation must come from feedback depth and outcomes.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: The cohort size, letter counts and guarantee language are internal and unverified.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: The offer does not rely on subsidy claims.

positioning_risk.reason: The product must justify why buyers should pay instead of using official free resources.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: Learners need OET Grade B to register with DHA or DOH or migrate to the UK or Australia.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: The reviewed sources support acceptance of OET but not the exact threshold or universal route requirement stated by the card.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: The curriculum requires 15-24 corrected letters and a max cohort of 10.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: These are internal course-design choices, not public facts.
A guarantee is not externally supported and may be risky marketing language.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Keep claims about registration and migration narrow enough to match official recognition language.
Decide whether a pass guarantee is product policy or should be removed from customer-facing copy.

sources.title: What is the OET Test, where and how do I take it
